meta_principle.name: Logic & Logical Consistency
meta_principle.description: The supreme principle from which all others derive.
rules: All organizational decisions must be logically consistent
When principles conflict, logical analysis determines resolution
Contradictions are impermissible and must be resolved
All principles below are valid insofar as they serve logical coherence

principles.name: Free & Open Source
principles.derivation: Open source enables verification, which is a logical necessity for validating correctness.
constraints: Tools and templates must be freely available

principles.name: Privacy & Security First
principles.derivation: Security failures create logical impossibilities - compromised systems cannot reliably execute intended logic.
constraints: Sensitive information must not leave the organization without consent
Default to secure practices in all workflows

principles.name: Support Many Use Cases
principles.derivation: Diverse use cases provide more data points for logical validation; flexibility is logically more robust than rigidity.
constraints: Templates and workflows should be flexible and adaptable
Support standard GitHub features where applicable

principles.name: Beginner-friendly & Human-readable
principles.derivation: Clear communication is a logical necessity; ambiguity prevents verification and introduces errors.
constraints: Documentation must be accessible to newcomers

principles.name: Deterministic & Reliable
principles.derivation: Determinism is a direct expression of logical consistency; same inputs must yield same outputs.
constraints: Workflows must produce consistent, predictable results

principles.name: Safe Execution
principles.derivation: Unsafe execution creates unpredictable states; predictability is a logical requirement for reliable systems.
constraints: No arbitrary code execution in templates or workflows

principles.name: Performance Matters
principles.derivation: Poor performance creates logical limits - infinite time requirements are logically equivalent to impossibility.

principles.name: Quality Over Quantity
principles.derivation: Logical value maximization requires prioritizing high-impact contributions over volume.

principles.name: Community Collaboration
principles.derivation: Diverse perspectives strengthen logical analysis through multiple validation paths.

principles.name: Transparency
principles.derivation: Transparency is required for verification; hidden logic cannot be validated.
constraints: Decisions and changes must be communicated openly

principles.name: Backward Compatibility
principles.derivation: Logical respect for existing dependencies; stability enables predictability.

principles.name: Security & Privacy Standards
principles.derivation: Security is prerequisite for reliability; privacy is logical consequence of autonomy.
constraints: Maintain high standards for data privacy

principles.name: Interoperability
principles.derivation: Logical systems must integrate; isolated systems create logical barriers.
constraints: Templates must work across different teams

principles.name: Clarity & Precision
principles.derivation: Ambiguity violates logical determinacy; precision is required for logical validity.
constraints: Clear, non-ambiguous definitions in all documentation

principles.name: Inclusivity
principles.derivation: Maximizing participant pool increases logical validation opportunities.

principles.name: Stability
principles.derivation: Stable foundations enable reliable logical reasoning over time.
constraints: Changes must not break existing workflows
